title: Top Scenario: Development And Engineering Delivery
summary: Development is the highest-signal workflow cluster across current public skill catalogs. Open Agent Skills shows `639` skills in Development, while Skills Hub foregrounds Build, Test, QA, Review, Deploy, Docs, Ops, Combo, and Meta categories for engineering teams.
market_evidence: Open Agent Skills category count: Development 639
Skills Hub category coverage: Build, Test, QA, Review, Deploy, Docs, Ops, Combo, Meta
Skills Hub popular skills: code-review, workflow-builder, design-to-code

title: Top Scenario: Office Document Automation
summary: Office and document workflows are one of the clearest marketplace categories. Open Agent Skills shows `181` skills in Document Processing, and Skills Hub prominently surfaces MarkItDown plus docs and accessibility workflows.
market_evidence: Open Agent Skills category count: Document Processing 181
Skills Hub category coverage: Docs, Accessibility, Productivity
Skills Hub popular skills: markitdown

title: Top Scenario: AI Agent Workflow Builder
summary: Workflow composition and skill orchestration are a distinct marketplace need. Skills Hub highlights workflow-builder, Skill Finder, and cross-tool installation, while this repo itself centers on reusable workflow assets.
market_evidence: Skills Hub engineer workflow: workflow-builder
Skills Hub popular orchestrator: Skill Finder
Skills Hub claims cross-tool skill install and composition on homepage

title: Top Scenario: Product Management And Team Collaboration
summary: Product and collaboration workflows are a strong secondary cluster. Open Agent Skills shows `373` Collaboration skills, while Skills Hub exposes Product, Productivity, and Team Lead flows like daily standups and team training.
market_evidence: Open Agent Skills category count: Collaboration 373
Skills Hub category coverage: Product, Productivity, Education
Skills Hub featured role skill: daily-standup

title: Top Scenario: Research And Investment Analysis
summary: Research, analysis, and investment-style workflows have clear demand in public catalogs. Open Agent Skills shows `195` skills in Data and Analysis, and Skills Hub highlights Research, Analysis, and Business-oriented categories suitable for market and investment work.
market_evidence: Open Agent Skills category count: Data and Analysis 195
Skills Hub category coverage: Analysis, Data, Research, Business
Skills Hub featured stack skill: research

title: Top Scenario: Security And Risk Review
summary: Security is one of the largest public skill categories. Open Agent Skills shows `408` Security skills, and Skills Hub ships a dedicated Security Hardening Kit centered on review and scanning workflows.
market_evidence: Open Agent Skills category count: Security 408
Skills Hub category coverage: Security
Skills Hub featured stack skill: security-review

title: Top Scenario: Cloud Deployment And Ops
summary: Deployment and operations remain a core engineering scenario. Skills Hub explicitly surfaces Deploy and Ops categories, and the imported vendor catalogs include optimization and deploy-oriented skills from Vercel and NVIDIA.
market_evidence: Skills Hub category coverage: Deploy, Ops
Skills Hub developer pipelines combine build, deploy, and review
Existing imported vendor skills: vercel-optimize, aiq-deploy

title: Top Scenario: Marketing And Content Operations
summary: Marketing and content work is a visible marketplace scenario. Skills Hub surfaces Marketing, Creative, and Content Creator workflows, while community catalogs expose brand, writing, growth, and social-optimization skills.
market_evidence: Skills Hub category coverage: Marketing, Creative
Skills Hub featured role skill: content-creator
Existing imported community skills: content-research-writer, brand-guidelines, twitter-algorithm-optimizer

title: Top Scenario: Business Finance And Backoffice
summary: Business, finance, and backoffice workflows are explicitly represented in public marketplaces. Open Agent Skills shows `156` Business skills, and Skills Hub groups finance, legal, HR, compliance, and operations under Business.

Skills Hub category coverage: Business includes finance, legal, HR, compliance, operations

title: Top Scenario: Design Presentation And UI Production
summary: Design and presentation production are large creative workflow clusters. Open Agent Skills shows `219` Creative and Media skills, while Skills Hub highlights frontend design, UI systems, design-to-code, and presentation-oriented creation paths.

Skills Hub category coverage: Creative, UX

title: Hot Scenario: Productivity And Office Assistant
summary: Beyond formal document workflows, productivity and office-assistant automation appear as a distinct hot scenario. Open Agent Skills shows `72` Productivity skills, and Skills Hub highlights automation, standups, task tracking, and file organization as recurring workflows.
market_evidence: Open Agent Skills category count: Productivity 72
Skills Hub category coverage: Productivity
Skills Hub featured role skills: automation-builder, daily-standup

title: Hot Scenario: Testing QA And Release Readiness
summary: Testing and QA deserve their own scenario instead of being hidden inside generic development. Skills Hub explicitly breaks out Test and QA categories and includes unit-test and preflight in its starter bundles.
market_evidence: Skills Hub category coverage: Test, QA
Skills Hub featured stack skills: unit-test, preflight
Skills Hub engineering starter bundles combine test, review, security, deploy

title: Hot Scenario: Documentation And Knowledge Management
summary: Documentation and knowledge capture are hot support scenarios across marketplaces. Skills Hub has a dedicated Docs category, while imported community and engineering catalogs expose knowledge graph, skill sync, and document conversion workflows.

Open Agent Skills capability model highlights repeatable workflows and domain expertise

title: Hot Scenario: Founder Prototyping And No Code Build
summary: Founder and no-code building workflows are prominent in the public market. Skills Hub explicitly calls out founder and builder roles, with app-builder, automation-builder, and content-creator as high-signal starter workflows.

Skills Hub featured role skills: app-builder, automation-builder, content-creator

title: Hot Scenario: Integration And MCP Tooling
summary: Integration and connector workflows are a visible hot scenario in skill marketplaces. Skills Hub includes an Integration category, while imported catalogs expose MCP builder and command-creation skills that map directly to toolchain extension work.

Open Agent Skills popular filters include MCP

title: Hot Scenario: Education And Team Enablement
summary: Training and enablement are their own hot scenario, not just an afterthought of productivity. Skills Hub includes Education and team-training flows with workshop and course generation, which map well to repeatable internal enablement workflows.
